goal: Implement a deterministic provider-based risk engine for Intent Wallet Copilot, including transparent risk signals, mock/API fallback providers, pre-sign simulation, Helius receipt support, and Safety Review UI disclosure.

tasks.title: Specify risk contracts and TDD fixtures
acceptanceCriteria: Types exist for RiskAssessment, RiskLevel, RiskReason, TokenSecurityData, LiquidityData, QuoteRiskData, RecipientRiskData, SimulationRiskData.
RiskReason/risk signal includes source, value, threshold, risk impact, and human-readable explanation.
Provider result types allow success, unavailable, failed, and mock/demo labeling.
Tests are written before implementation for aggregation and provider behavior.
evidence: Extended RiskReason type with checkName, source, value, threshold, riskImpact, explanation, isMock fields
Added RiskAssessment.providerResults and signals alias
Added all required data types: TokenSecurityData, LiquidityData, QuoteRiskData, RecipientRiskData, SimulationRiskData, TransactionReceipt
Added RiskProvider interface and RiskProviderInput/Result types
Created aggregation.test.ts with 8 test cases for aggregation rules (RED phase)
Created providers.test.ts with mock provider tests (RED phase)
Implemented aggregation.ts with deterministic risk level aggregation (GREEN phase)
Implemented MockTokenSecurityProvider and MockRiskScoreProvider (GREEN phase)
Added vitest config and test scripts to package.json
Validation: npm test -- --run passed 40 tests across 6 files.

tasks.title: Implement local deterministic providers and mocks
acceptanceCriteria: LocalAllowlistProvider blocks unknown symbols, unknown mints, and mint mismatches.
RecipientValidationProvider blocks invalid public keys, unresolved .sol names, and unknown contact names; new valid addresses are MEDIUM; saved contacts are LOW.
Mock providers return deterministic demo data and are clearly labeled mock/demo.
Tests cover LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, and BLOCKED outputs where applicable.
evidence: Created localProviders.test.ts with 10 test cases covering allowlist and recipient validation (RED phase)
Implemented LocalAllowlistProvider enforcing SOL/USDC/BONK/JUP/PYTH allowlist with BLOCKED for unknown tokens
Implemented RecipientValidationProvider with PublicKey validation, .sol blocking, contact name blocking, new address MEDIUM, saved contact LOW
Mock providers from T1 include isMock flag and clear demo data labeling
All providers follow RiskProvider interface contract

tasks.title: Implement external/API-capable providers with safe fallbacks
acceptanceCriteria: Jupiter quote risk enforces no route/invalid output BLOCKED, price impact >10 HIGH, >3 MEDIUM, slippage >5 HIGH, >2 MEDIUM.
Birdeye token security rules enforce age, liquidity, holder count, concentration, verification, and mint/freeze authority thresholds.
External risk score provider maps critical/severe/rug/poor to HIGH, medium to MEDIUM, unavailable to non-blocking informational signal.
No API secret is read or exposed; missing keys use mock/demo providers or unavailable signals.
evidence: Created externalProviders.test.ts with tests for quote risk, price impact, slippage, and fallback behavior (RED phase)
Implemented JupiterQuoteRiskProvider with all threshold rules: no route BLOCKED, price impact >10 HIGH />3 MEDIUM, slippage >5 HIGH />2 MEDIUM
Implemented BirdeyeTokenSecurityProvider with import.meta.env.VITE_BIRDEYE_API_KEY check and fallback to MockTokenSecurityProvider
Implemented ExternalRiskScoreProvider with import.meta.env.VITE_RISK_SCORE_API_KEY check and fallback to MockRiskScoreProvider
All providers check for API keys without exposing values and gracefully fall back to mock data
Birdeye provider now calls configured Birdeye API defensively and falls back to mock on unavailable/error.
External risk score provider calls configured VITE_RISK_SCORE_API_URL defensively and falls back to mock/unavailable signals.

tasks.title: Implement transaction simulation and receipt providers
acceptanceCriteria: Prepared transactions are simulated before wallet signing.
Simulation failure creates BLOCKED signal and prevents signing.
Simulation not-yet-run is visible in the Safety Review before preparation.
Helius receipt provider returns enhanced receipt when configured and basic explorer receipt when unavailable.
evidence: Created simulationReceipt.test.ts with tests for simulation states and receipt fallback (RED phase)
Implemented TransactionSimulationProvider with not-yet-run LOW signal, simulation failure BLOCKED, success LOW
Implemented HeliusReceiptProvider with import.meta.env.VITE_HELIUS_API_KEY check
Receipt provider always returns a receipt: enhanced when Helius available, basic with explorer URL when not
Explorer URLs use devnet/mainnet cluster based on VITE_SOLANA_NETWORK
TransactionSimulationProvider returns visible not-yet-run LOW signal and BLOCKED on simulation failure.
HeliusReceiptProvider calls configured Helius enhanced transactions endpoint when key is present; otherwise returns basic explorer receipt.

tasks.title: Implement deterministic risk aggregation engine
acceptanceCriteria: If any signal is BLOCKED, final risk is BLOCKED.
Else if any signal is HIGH, final risk is HIGH.
Else if two or more signals are MEDIUM, final risk is HIGH.
Else if one signal is MEDIUM, final risk is MEDIUM.
Else final risk is LOW.
The engine is deterministic and contains no LLM/AI risk logic.
evidence: Created riskEngine.test.ts with integration tests for provider orchestration (RED phase)
Refactored riskEngine.ts to orchestrate all 6 providers asynchronously
Uses aggregateRiskLevel() from aggregation.ts implementing exact rules: BLOCKED > HIGH > 2+ MEDIUM > 1 MEDIUM > LOW
All provider results collected in RiskAssessment.providerResults
Backward compatibility maintained with RiskReason format and signals alias
No LLM/AI logic - purely deterministic rule-based aggregation
assessRisk is now async to support provider orchestration

tasks.title: Integrate Safety Review UI transparency
acceptanceCriteria: Safety Review includes a 'How we checked this' section.
Each signal shows check name, source/tool, result/value, risk impact, threshold, and explanation.
Mock/demo/unavailable checks are visibly labeled.
Existing recommendation and high-risk confirmation flow still work.
evidence: Added expandable 'How We Checked This' section with ChevronDown/Up toggle
Created RiskSignalDetail component showing checkName, source, result, risk impact, threshold, explanation
Mock/demo signals display DEMO DATA badge with FlaskConical icon
formatValue helper formats numbers, booleans, and nulls for display
Existing reasons/recommendation UI preserved and enhanced
All existing risk confirmation flows maintained

tasks.title: Wire risk engine into app flow and history
acceptanceCriteria: Initial assessment runs asynchronously with provider signals.
Prepared transfer transaction is simulated before sendTransaction is called.
BLOCKED simulation stops signing and updates UI/history.
Post-confirmation receipt is fetched and stored/displayed as history metadata when possible.
evidence: Updated handleIntentSubmit to await assessRisk() asynchronously before saving history
Updated handlePrepare to build tx, then call assessRisk with preparedTransaction and connection
Simulation results update riskAssessment state before sendTransaction
BLOCKED simulation stops flow, sets error, and marks history as failed
After confirmation, HeliusReceiptProvider.fetchReceipt() is called
Receipt stored in history entry's receipt field when available
Receipt fetch errors are non-critical and logged

tasks.title: Document environment configuration and validate build/tests
acceptanceCriteria: .env.example lists only variable names/placeholders for optional APIs.
npm test, npm run build, and npm run lint are attempted and results recorded.
docs/task-breakdown.json statuses/evidence are updated.
No secret values are committed or printed.
evidence: Created .env.example with placeholder variable names only; file not read back because safe-secrets guardrail treats .env* as protected.
Created .gitignore to prevent .env files from being committed.
Added Vitest test scripts/dependencies and vitest.config.ts.
npm install --registry=https://registry.npmjs.org/ partially timed out but installed node_modules sufficient for validation.
npm test -- --run passed: 6 test files, 40 tests.
npm run build passed; Vite produced dist successfully with existing bundle-size warnings.
npm run lint completed with 0 errors and 6 pre-existing react-refresh warnings in UI primitives.
npx tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit was attempted and failed on pre-existing third-party/UI typing issues unrelated to this risk-engine change.
No secret values were read, printed, or committed.

tasks.title: Replace demo-only provider stubs with real public/API implementations and fallback labeling
acceptanceCriteria: JupiterQuoteRiskProvider fetches a real Jupiter quote when configured/available instead of only inspecting demo quote data.
BirdeyeTokenSecurityProvider performs a real HTTP request when VITE_BIRDEYE_API_KEY is present and falls back to clearly labeled mock data when unavailable.
ExternalRiskScoreProvider performs a real HTTP request when VITE_RISK_SCORE_API_URL is configured and falls back/mock-labels when unavailable.
HeliusReceiptProvider performs a real enhanced transaction request when VITE_HELIUS_API_KEY is present and falls back to basic receipt when unavailable.
No secret values are read or printed; only VITE_* variable names are referenced.
Tests cover real-fetch success paths using mocked fetch and missing-key fallback paths.
evidence: JupiterQuoteRiskProvider now fetches real quotes from Jupiter Quote API (default: https://quote-api.jup.ag/v6)
Uses TOKEN_REGISTRY to get mints and decimals, converts amounts to raw units (multiply by 10^decimals)
Parses priceImpactPct, routePlan (single or multi-hop), outAmount, otherAmountThreshold from API response
Falls back to demo quote (input.quote) when API fetch fails, ensuring app works without API access
BirdeyeTokenSecurityProvider already had real API implementation; enhanced with detailed comments explaining fallback
ExternalRiskScoreProvider already had real API implementation; enhanced with detailed comments explaining fallback
HeliusReceiptProvider already had real API implementation; enhanced with detailed comments explaining fallback
Added comprehensive tests with mocked fetch covering: successful API calls, HIGH/MEDIUM price impact parsing, multi-hop routes, fallback behavior
Added tests for Birdeye, ExternalRiskScore, and Helius providers verifying real fetch paths and fallback paths
Created docs/api-provider-fallbacks.md documenting when real API vs demo data is used and why
Added inline comments to all providers explaining fallback strategy: graceful degradation when APIs unavailable
All providers use only VITE_* prefixed environment variables; no secrets are read or printed
npm test -- --run: 52 tests passed (6 files)
npm run build: succeeded (dist generated successfully)
npm run lint: 0 errors, 6 pre-existing warnings in UI primitives (not modified)
Implemented real Jupiter Quote API path in JupiterQuoteRiskProvider using TOKEN_REGISTRY mints/decimals and raw amount conversion.
Default Jupiter endpoint updated to https://lite-api.jup.ag/swap/v1 from current docs; VITE_JUPITER_API_URL can override.
Parsed Jupiter priceImpactPct, routePlan, outAmount, slippageBps, and otherAmountThreshold response fields defensively.
Fixed tester-reviewer C1 finding by renaming slippageBps parameter to slippagePercent.
Added HTTP error fallback test for Jupiter API 429/failed responses.
Birdeye, external risk score, and Helius providers have real fetch paths when configured and fallback paths when unavailable.
npm test -- --run passed: 6 files, 53 tests.
npm run build passed.
npm run lint: 0 errors, 6 pre-existing UI primitive warnings.

tasks.title: Validate functional run after real API implementation
acceptanceCriteria: npm test -- --run passes
npm run build passes
npm run lint has no new errors
Tester-reviewer report validates real API paths and fallback behavior
evidence: Tester-reviewer returned CONDITIONAL PASS for T9 before follow-up cleanup.
Follow-up cleanup fixed critical naming issue C1 (slippagePercent).
Added HTTP error fallback test; total tests now 53 passing.
npm test -- --run passed.
npm run build passed.
npm run lint completed with 0 errors and 6 pre-existing warnings.
